images.caption: Figure 1:Left:Accuracy on AIME24. CE loss shows great degradation, while policy loss initially surpasses but degrades as training progresses. In contrast, OCC sustains gains throughout training and consistently outperforms.Middle & Right:Illustrations of the MTP training regimes. Detach training stops the gradient from MTP into the main model. Joint MTP-RL training allows this gradient to flow back into the main model, enabling joint updates.

Figure 3:Evolution of the policy-aligned gain across training steps (Eq. (10)). Each curve is a downward-opening parabola inλ\lambda, coloured from early (dark purple) to late (bright yellow) steps. The vertical dashed line marks a fixedλ\lambda: at early steps, the intersection point ofλ\lambdaand the parabola lies above zero (positive gain), but as training progresses the parabola drifts so that the intersection point falls under zero (negative gain).
Table 1:Main results on mathematical reasoning benchmarks across models and algorithms. We report avg@32 accuracy (%). Best results are inbold; second best areunderlined.
Figure 4:Curve of the absolute value of OCC coefficientλt\lambda_{t}.
Figure 4:Curve of the absolute value of OCC coefficientλt\lambda_{t}.
Figure 5:Wall-clock training time of OCC, Detach and Full-model.
Figure 6:Training reward of CE Loss, Detach, Policy Loss and OCC.
Figure 7:Training reward of the Policy Loss with different coefficient.

Figure 8:Relationship betwen the log-probability proxies and the true full-model gradient quantities. Each point corresponds to one training step.Left:the alignment proxy plotted against the full-model first-order correlation.Right:the variance proxy plotted against the full-model second-order penalty. The proxies exhibit highly positive correlation with the true gradient quantities (rris annotated above).
